characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Adaptational Badass: Bunniculato an extent.In the books, Bunnicula's vampire traits, apart from his eating habits, were largely anInformed Attributeand left up to the reader's imagination. This show pretty much outright states that Bunnicula is a vampire and suitably gives him some powers such as turning his ears into wings forFlight, detaching his limbs and having them move on their own, and moderateSuper-Strength. Adaptational Jerkass: He's not a bad sort, but the series's Bunnicula is mischievous and somewhat vindictive. This is in contrast to the Bunnicula of the novels, who was a timidShrinking Violetwho only caused trouble by draining vegetables, and otherwise was benign and quiet. Adaptation Personality Change: Thanks to the series eschewing theMaybe Magic, Maybe Mundaneapproach of the books, Bunnicula is far less shy about being a vampire. He’s also much more mischievous and jocular than his book counterpart, who was more of a gentleShrinking Violet. Badass Adorable: Bunnicula, a vampire rabbit who is usually the one to correct the problems in the episode, all while still looking cute as a button. Berserk Button: He doesn't like when people or animals eating his carrots without his permission.

characters.name: Bunnicula
characters.description: Adaptational Badass: Bunniculato an extent.In the books, Bunnicula's vampire traits, apart from his eating habits, were largely anInformed Attributeand left up to the reader's imagination. This show pretty much outright states that Bunnicula is a vampire and suitably gives him some powers such as turning his ears into wings forFlight, detaching his limbs and having them move on their own, and moderateSuper-Strength. Adaptational Jerkass: He's not a bad sort, but the series's Bunnicula is mischievous and somewhat vindictive. This is in contrast to the Bunnicula of the novels, who was a timidShrinking Violetwho only caused trouble by draining vegetables, and otherwise was benign and quiet. Adaptation Personality Change: Thanks to the series eschewing theMaybe Magic, Maybe Mundaneapproach of the books, Bunnicula is far less shy about being a vampire. He’s also much more mischievous and jocular than his book counterpart, who was more of a gentleShrinking Violet. Badass Adorable: Bunnicula, a vampire rabbit who is usually the one to correct the problems in the episode, all while still looking cute as a button. Berserk Button: He doesn't like when people or animals eating his carrots without his permission.

characters.name: Chester
characters.description: Act of True Love: In "Evil Cat Video", when Chester is ordered to scratch Mina by a ghost mouse, his love for Mina is so strong, he manages to stop himself from hurting her. Adaptational Intelligence: In the books, while Chester was the first to catch on that Bunnicula was a vampire, he was also very clearly out of his mind and more book-smart than practical. He often became convinced that obviously mundane things were the work of the supernatural, made a lot of obviously terrible decisions, and was stubbornly convinced Bunnicula was plotting a vampire uprising despite no evidence backing him up - and a lot of it proving him wrong. He’s a more traditional straight man here and the voice of reason. Adaptational Nice Guy: The Chester of the books was a snob who constantly bullied Harold and was prone to fits of homicidal paranoia. Chester here is much more amicable and rational; he doesn't get along with Bunnicula, but it's difficult to see this Chester trying to outright kill him. Adaptation Personality Change: He lacks the snobbishness and irrationality of the books' Chester. He's more of a down-to-earth, albeit neurotic, straight man here. Adaptation Species Change: In the original books, Chester is a tabby cat. However, in the series, he's designed to look like a Siamese, despite his tail not being brown and being slightly bushy.

characters.name: Harold
characters.description: Adaptational Dumbass: Harold in the original books/novels was a laid-back, scruffy, and loving older dog that was under the Monroe's care and spent afternoons on the sofa. However, he was pretty clearly smart and rational, just less cultured and snobbish than Chester. In the Cartoon Network adaption, he is more of a goofball than his previous portrayals and is a straightforward idiot. Adaptation Personality Change: In the books, Harold was uncultured and laid-back, but was clearly theOnly Sane Manand the voice of reason who was leagues more mature than Chester. Here, he's a straightforwardKindhearted Simpleton. All Love Is Unrequited: In "Vampire Rabbit Season", he develops romantic feelings for Fluffy the Vampire Pointer, but she's not into him. The Big Guy: To Chester & Bunnicula. Brainwashed and Crazy: In "Vampire Tick", he was turn into a mindless obedient dog by a vampire tick and make him eat anything even living animals.

characters.name: Mina Monroe
characters.description: Alliterative Name:MinaMonroe. Animal Lover: Mina loves animals and treats them kindly with love and care. She gets along with her pets very well and loves them equally. Distaff Counterpart: She is the female counterpart of Toby Monroe. Genki Girl: Mina can be this at times when cuddling Bunnicula and telling him how much she loves him. Imaginary Friend: In "My Imaginary Friend", she had an imaginary friend named Hamburger Cheese when she was three years old. She stopped playing with him when she met Chester and Harold when she was five.

characters.name: Arthur Monroe
characters.description: Always a Child to Parent: Mina's dad throws her a party for her 13th birthday...one better suited to a five-year-old still in her princess and pony phase. Bumbling Dad: To some extent, but stilldevotedand caring to Mina. The Faceless: Up until theSeries Finale, his face was always hidden from view.

characters.name: Marie Monroe
characters.description: Determinator: Motivated to leave her key to her great-niece. Satellite Character: Other than being Mina's aunt, Marie doesn't really have as many appearances and screen time as other characters.

characters.name: Becky
characters.description: Deadpan Snarker: Sheisthis since she is very sarcastic towards her friends and has a dry sense of humor. Loony Fan: Loves gnomes a bit too much. Only Sane Woman: More perceptive than the optimistic and cheery Mina. But not as perceptive as Marsha, however. Valley Girl: For all of her snarks, she isn't as smart as Marsha is.

characters.name: Marsha
characters.description: A supporting character who is one of Mina's best friends and Lugosi's owner. The Cynic: Pretty pessimistic. Foil: Mina is the foil to Marsha. While Marsha's timid and savvy, Mina's hyperactive and energetic. Hyper-Competent Sidekick: What Marsha lacks in winning Mina and Becky's respect, she makes up for it in being a true friend. Love Interest: To Scott, in which she is more comfortable being around since she has a big crush on him and they both share some things in common such asbeing goofyand liking pancakes.

characters.name: Scott Dingleman
characters.description: Brainwashed and Crazy: When he was brainwashed and had amnesia, he became pretty naive and had a "mind like a sponge" analogy. The Confidant: In the"Scott Free"episode, he figures out Bunnicula's secret before getting amnesia, but becomes smarter because of being able to talk to the pets. When he is cured and returns to normal, he still knows Bunnicula's secret and understands the pets, but he promises to keep it safe. Insecure Love Interest: He appears to have a shy side when it comes to liking Mina back, as he was worried when he sent a flirty emoji to his crush, Mina Monroe, and thought she might get the wrong idea, not knowing she likes him as well. Mellow Fellow: Quite laid-back. Nice Guy: Pretty affable.

characters.name: Patches the Weredude
characters.description: A laidback, fun-loving weredude who acts like a cat at all times and is friends with Chester, Bunnicula, and Harold. Befriending the Enemy: Becomes friends with Steve, who was also aHalf-Human Hybridof a rat that Patches was trying to capture originally. Buffoonish Tomcat: A fun-lovingHalf-Human Hybridversion of this due to how forgetful and slow-witted he can be. Cloudcuckoolander: Has an oddball and goofy appearance and shares a similar kind of being just as slow-witted as much asHaroldis. Delusions Of Cathood: Acts like a cat even in human form, just enough for anyone to see him as anextremely eccentric person and/or a weirdo, Mina is a prime example of who sees him as this.

characters.name: Lugosi
characters.description: Ax-Crazy: A pretty crazy guinea-pig. Undying Loyalty: Very helpful towards the main characters andwantsto serve Bunnicula as he worships the very ground that Bunnicula walks on, andwill do anything to please his "master", and shuns anybody who isn’t devoted or loyal towards Bunnicula.

characters.name: Rusty Bones
characters.description: The Eeyore: A perpetually depressed ghost. Failure Is the Only Option: When he wanted to haunt Mina and put her through his hard life, he gave up after he discovered she wasimmune to being depressed. Only Sane Man: He can be pretty rational. Not Evil, Just Misunderstood: He is a ghost that tries to haunt others, but is one who is very depressed and doesn't really succeed at that due to how miserable he is instead. Singing Voice Dissonance: Sings a lot about his awful life.

characters.name: Fluffy
characters.description: A Doberman Pinscher who hunts vampires and was originally an enemy to Bunnicula. Later, however, she became another close ally. Affectionate Parody: Fluffy is a parody ofBuffy the Vampire Slayer. Determinator: Fluffy always seems to be very diligent and focused on her job at hand. She tends to get annoyed when anyone gets in her way (e.gHarold) or a plan she has fails. More than likely, finishing her tasks at hand is all she worries about. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: Fluffy initially hated all monsters and was resolved to hunt them down. To that end, she tried to hunt down Bunnicula throughout the episode until she realized he cares about his family. From this display of affection, Fluffy sees that not all monsters are evil. Serious Business: She seems to be very strict and serious.

characters.name: Baron Karloff
characters.description: A French Bulldog with his last name related to Boris Karloff, he once tried to summon a grave walker named Rip so that Fluffy can fight him and win because he is an obsessed fan of Fluffy's work and tried to get a vampire to bite him so he could be a vampire Fluffy can defeat for him. Affably Evil: Became a villain that Fluffy can be friendly to (but not as much) and one she mostly ignores. Cursed with Awesome: He may have turned himself into a vampire, but he's got some cool skills to use with what he was cursed with such as summoning Rip using his cursed staff. He presumably has the same powers as other vampires like Bunnicula and Count Orlock. Devoted to You: A huge fan of Fluffy and is somewhat protective of her, as seen when he tried to stop Rip from killing Fluffy. Dogs Are Dumb:InvertedandAvertedbecause Baron isn't at all dumb; he was just misled and became a villain out of being ignored, as Baron shows a nice amount of intelligence from time to time.

characters.name: Bunnicula's Bride
characters.description: Mina's creation that came to life with electroshock Berserk Button: Do not interrupt her moment with Bunnicula, because she's not going to be easy with that. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: At first glance, she seems like a nice girl, but behind that façade, she's a completely insane villain. Yandere: Her relationship with Bunnicula is pretty toxic.

characters.name: Toby Monroe
characters.description: The Baby of the Bunch: The younger of the brothers and the kindest. Tastes Like Friendship: Likes sharing his snacks (especially chocolate) with his dog Harold.

characters.name: Pete Monroe
characters.description: Big Brother Bully: He's often this towards Toby, though mostly just verbal putdowns and occasional roughhousing that their parents never allow to go too far. Hypocrite: InThe Celery Stalks At Midnight, Pete dismisses his family's usual habit of talking to their pets as being pointless because "they're just dumb animals", but he himself had never previously had any issue talking to them in that manner. Harold wonders if he's just going through a contrarian phase. Know-Nothing Know-It-All: In the first book, he very confidently asserts that the obvious cause of the white vegetables is due to them being "non-organic" and having lots of DDT. InNighty-Nightmare, meanwhile, he constantly tries to take charge of the family's camping trip, citing his skills as a Boy Scout, but as he is only eleven and hasn't earned the merit badge yet - not to mention a littletooeager for something bad to happen so he can save the day - his mother retains a healthy skepticism and refuses to allow them to take the risks Pete advocates.

characters.name: Howie
characters.description: Adapted Out: Is a main character in all the books except the first two and the point-of-view character for the ''Tales from the House of Bunnicula" series, but never appears in any of the adaptations. Not even theAnimated Adaptationfrom 2016. Dogs Are Dumb: An Dachshundpuppywho is kind of slow. Interspecies Adoption: A rare instance of the child adopting the parent - for whatever reasons of his own. Howie, early on, latched onto Chester as his "Pop", and looks up to him as a parental figure. Chester eventually just becomes resigned to it. Mellow Fellow: Quite a carefree pup. Our Werewolves Are Different: Well, Chester's the only one who thinks so, but the only "evidence" he has of his theory that Howie is a dachshund/werewolf hybrid is his unusually loud, spooky howls (which he shares with his parents).
